title: podman machine init fails after fresh install
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
it's impossible to do podman machine init

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. brew install podman

2. podman machine init


**Describe the results you received:**
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-36.20221014.2.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2.xz: done 
Extracting compressed file Error: key generation failed, unable to read from stderr: exit status 1

**Describe the results you expected:**
podman successfully inits

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
podman can not even start, so version comes from `brew info podman`

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
==> podman: stable 4.3.0 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
/opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.3.0 (185 files, 47.6MB) *
  Poured from bottle on 2022-10-23 at 18:03:55
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0 and GPL-3.0-or-later
==> Dependencies
Build: go-md2man ✘, go@1.18 ✘
Required: qemu ✔
==> Options
--HEAD
        Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
zsh completions have been installed to:
  /opt/homebrew/share/zsh/site-functions

To restart podman after an upgrade:
  brew services restart podman
Or, if you don't want/need a background service you can just run:
  /opt/homebrew/opt/podman/bin/podman system service --time=0
==> Analytics
install: 20,540 (30 days), 68,225 (90 days), 214,776 (365 days)
install-on-request: 19,693 (30 days), 66,167 (90 days), 212,314 (365 days)
build-error: 0 (30 days)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
sw_vers yields: 
```
ProductName:    macOS
ProductVersion: 12.6
BuildVersion:   21G115
```

comments.body: Looks like an SSH key generation issue, do you know if you're using a standard SSH on your machine?

would having 
```
-rw-------   1 duuude  staff   411B 25 jul 19:55 podman-machine-default
-rw-r--r--   1 duuude  staff   100B 25 jul 19:55 podman-machine-default.pub
```
in $HOME/.ssh/ cause the issue?

ok, 
i deleted both above files, then I ran the following: 
```
    ~/.ssh  podman machine init                                                                                                                                                                                               ✔  20:39:18   
Error: podman-machine-default: VM already exists

     ~/.ssh  podman machine init stop                                                                                                                                                                                      125 ✘  20:39:22   
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start stop


     ~/.ssh  podman machine start                                                                                                                                                                                     ✔  21s    20:39:52   
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: dial unix /var/folders/h8/xz0mt9n94894bvvpfq0c2jhm0000gn/T/podman/qmp_podman-machine-default.sock: connect: no such file or directory

     ~/.ssh  podman machine stop                                                                                                                                                                                   125 ✘  3s    20:40:47   
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully

     ~/.ssh  podman machine start                                                                                                                                                                                              ✔  20:40:57   
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Error: dial unix /var/folders/h8/xz0mt9n94894bvvpfq0c2jhm0000gn/T/podman/qmp_podman-machine-default.sock: connect: no such file or directory

     ~/.ssh  rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers                                                                                                                                                                                  ✔  20:41:18   

     ~/.ssh  podman machine init                                                                                                                                                                                               ✔  20:41:30   
Error: podman-machine-default: VM already exists

     ~/.ssh  !4329                                                                                                                                                                                                         125 ✘  20:41:35   

     ~/.ssh  rm -rf ~/.config/containers/                                                                                                                                                                                  125 ✘  20:41:50   

     ~/.ssh  podman machine init                                                                                                                                                                                               ✔  20:41:52   
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-36.20221014.2.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2.xz: done  
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start


     ~/.ssh  podman machine start                                                                                                                                                                                     ✔  38s    20:42:33   
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Mounting volume... /Users/duuude:/Users/duuude


```

Now it works, so, something went wrong with the fact that the ssh key pair having the same name was already in $HOME/.ssh 
Does that mean anything to you ? Is that ok by you ? I mean, probably it should erase that keypair shouldn't it ? I mean, it's like a reserved name for the keypair? 
How do you approach this question?? 
Thanks

I'm having this issue from time to time as well.
Deleting the machine and performing again `podman machine init` is working

it's hard to reproduce as it happens from time to time.

```
$ podman machine init foo
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-36.20221030.2.1-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz: done
Extracting compressed file
Error: key generation failed, unable to read from stderr: exit status 1

$ podman machine init foo
Error: foo: VM already exists

$ podman machine rm foo

The following files will be deleted:

/Users/benoitf/.ssh/foo
/Users/benoitf/.ssh/foo.pub
/Users/benoitf/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/foo.ign
/Users/benoitf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/foo_fedora-coreos-36.20221030.2.1-qemu.x86_64.qcow2
/Users/benoitf/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/foo/podman.sock
/Users/benoitf/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/foo.json


Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y


$ podman machine init foo
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

	podman machine start foo
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Are we still seeing this?  Perhaps the problem is in qemu on a Mac.

Well, it’s more related to ssh keys in home directory rather than a problem in qemu but are you suggesting qemu is managing those keys?
I worked around the problem by deleting manually the key pair in my ssh folder… probably not the right way to solve It I guess ?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I don't see a way to handle this properly so I am closing, since you were able to solve the problem.
